text: Text classification is a basic task in natural language processing (NLP) that usually needs feature engineering by using dictionaries, knowledge bases or complex kernels. In this paper, we design a novel model named Recurrent Convolutional Neural Network (RCNN) to replace the feature engineering work by taking the advantages of recurrent neural network (RNN) and convolutional neural network (CNN). The designed model uses a bi-directional RNN to learn the word embeddings while reducing the noise in the word embeddings by comparing different context windows, which is proven to be less than the standard window-based neural networks. A max-pooling layer is used to select the most representative features for the whole text. The experimental results show that our model achieves the best performance on four commonly used datasets and surpasses all the state-of-the-art results, especially for the document-level classification. In addition, our model enjoys a linear time complexity regarding the input length, which supports the high performance on texts of various lengths.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
